steps.title: Define variables in terms of mass B
steps.content:
Let mass of A = 4.38B (since 438% = 4.38).
Let mass of A = 0.00073C (since 0.073% = 0.00073).
Equate the two expressions for A: $$4.38B = 0.00073C$$

steps.title: Solve for C in terms of B
steps.content:
Substitute into equation: $$4.38B = 0.00073C \Rightarrow C = \frac{4.38}{0.00073}B$$
Simplify the division: $$\frac{4.38}{0.00073} = 6000$$
Thus: $$C = 6000B$$

steps.title: Convert to percentage and divide by 1000
steps.content:
Since C = 6000B, mass C is 6000% of mass B (p = 6000%).
Calculate $\frac{p}{1000}$: $$\frac{6000}{1000} = 600$$
The answer is 600.
